Consensus implied ~$53.85B (range $52.24–$55.47B) based on prior quarter $46.74B + trailing growth of +15.2% Q/Q.

Consensus implied ~$25.46B (range $24.70–$26.23B) based on prior quarter $22.10B + trailing growth of +15.2% Q/Q.

Customer concentration: Top 4 hyperscaler customers (Meta, Microsoft, Google, Amazon) drive ~50%+ of Data Center revenue — vendor consolidation or in-house ASIC adoption is a top tail risk.
Single-customer dependency: largest customer disclosed at ~17% of Q4 FY26 revenue.
Manufacturing & supply chain: Exclusive dependence on TSMC for advanced node (CoWoS / SiP / advanced packaging); supply constraints have been the bottleneck through FY25-FY26.
HBM memory dependence on Micron, SK Hynix, Samsung — competing for limited HBM4 capacity in FY27.
Geopolitical: US export controls to China — H20 ban (Apr 2025) cost ~$5.5B; further tightening risk persists.
Taiwan geopolitical risk; CHIPS Act conditionality.
Competition: AMD MI400 series ramping H2 2026; AVGO custom XPUs already shipping at hyperscalers.
In-house silicon at Google (TPU), Amazon (Trainium/Inferentia), Meta (MTIA) targeting >$15B run-rate by 2027.
Valuation & cycle: AI capex digestion risk in CY27 if hyperscaler ROI metrics disappoint.
P/E FY1 26× looks reasonable vs growth but assumes continued execution; deceleration could trigger compression.

fundamentals.risk_source: NVDA 10-K Item 1A (FY2026 filing, accession 0001045810-26-000021) plus 10-Q FY2026 quarterly filings
fundamentals.market_cap_note: $215.20 close on 5/8/26 × ~24,489M fully-diluted shares (post-10:1 stock split June 2024) = $5.27T fully-diluted market cap
fundamentals.audit_note: All values reconcile to SEC EDGAR XBRL company-facts API (CIK 0001045810, NVIDIA Corp) and to NVDA/Earnings/*.htm 10-Q/10-K filings (FY25 Q1 through FY26 Q4 10-K). EPS post-10:1 split (June 7, 2024). Capex per quarter estimated from CFS PP&E additions disclosure.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $68.13B in FY2026 Q4, +73.2% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $78.62B (+15.4% vs current Q) — set by 41 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $1.75 vs current $1.76.
Margin context: 63.1% net margin (65.0% op margin) — expansion likely.
Next earnings: 2026-05-20 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is constructive — consensus is achievable based on current run-rate.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $216B (+65.5% TTM YoY) — strong secular tailwind.
Sell-side: FY1 $368B → FY2 $486B (+32.0% multi-year top-line implied).
9-quarter run-rate CAGR: +75.6% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
FCF generation $34.8B/qtr funds long-term capital return optionality (buybacks/dividends/M&A).
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if growth durability + margin expansion both hold.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 47.0% across 13 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 13 dates spanning 97–944 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±23.6% ($164.38 – $266.02).
397-day implied move: ±49.0% ($109.77 – $320.63) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $270 (+25.4% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-6.0pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Buy 1-year LEAPS call at $194 (10% ITM, 70-delta proxy). Long-cycle trend up + sell-side sees +25.4% upside to $270 PT → ITM LEAPS gives ~80% of stock exposure at ~40% of capital, with limited downside vs holding.
